title: Cuisinart DCG-12BC Grind Central Coffee Grinder, Blade, Silver
description:
About this item
EASILY GRIND LARGE QUANTITIES: You'll easily process up to 90g/3.2oz of beans at once, enough to make up to 18 cups of fresh coffee
MODERN DESIGN: We bring a modern touches to this coffee grinder with popular brushed stainless steel to seamlessly blend with your appliances
HEAVY-DUTY MOTOR: Stainless steel blades and high power motor with provides consistent results with on/off control for simple operation
LID FEATURES: We designed this coffee grinder to have a built-in safety interlock to prevent operation unless lid is securely closed – store extra coffee grounds in the grinding bowl
EXTRA PERKS: Dishwasher-safe, removable stainless steel bowl and cord storage for ease of use. This product is BPA free and comes with an instruction book to assist with the set up and learning process
reviews_5s:
Great quality machine made of good material, easy to use and powerful. The size was perfect for what I needed. I love that you can control the speed and coarseness of the coffee grounds. Best of all it is not loud and super easy to clean!This is my second grinder that is just like this. Works so well. The last one lasted over 4 years grinding every week. Well worth buying!High qualityAfter five years, I am starting on my second Cuisinart Coffee Grinder. While on the surface that might not sound like a raving review, it is. I used my grinder every three days for five years and last week, despite the clear marker for full level, I overfilled it and burned up the motor. I accept full responsibility and I promptly replaced it with exactly the same model.

I tried to replace it a few months ago. Not because it doesn't grind perfectly, but because I have to stand there and hold the button down the entire time to run the motor. When I first bought it I tested grind times and found that I get the perfect consistency at three minutes. A safe button that requires you to hold it down is probably ideal to many, but I see it as three minutes I could spend eating my breakfast or feeding the dogs — three lost minutes. I bought several different coffee grinders and returned each one. They simply did a poor job on grinding the beans, even at their finest settings. (I make espresso, so I want an extremely fine grind and the other models just couldn't do it.)

For all you reviewers who complain about half-ground beans, try grinding for longer. It's a simple fix and is as designed.

I highly recommend this product and just about any other product with a Cuisinart logo. Good, sturdy, dependable equipment.Second attempt. First it was delivered as it was used, I returned and got a new one today. Very good machine, I had one before and lasted 2 years grinding great coffee.I've had my grinder since 2004 and I love it! I was going to buy one for a friend so I came to Amazon to do so and was shocked to see all of the people who don't like it! We are "weekend coffee drinkers" who only tend to grind beans once a week on weekends, when we are entertaining or just relaxing around the house. I am usually grinding beans for about 5 or 6 people at a time and I use it, as the instructions say, by grinding only 20 or 30 seconds at a time. I use it for grinding good quality beans to a coarse grind for use in my French Press.

I wonder if some of the people whose motors have started smoking or burned out have been keeping the motor running for a few minutes at a time? Or perhaps they have washed the cup in the dishwasher so much with hard water that there is a scale build up?

I like the fact that the grinder cup has a plastic lid, so that if I grind too much I can easily save it in the refrigerator.

I also like the fact that it is so easy to use and that you just press down on the bar on the top of the clear plastic lid to get it to grind. When you stop pressing the bar, the motor stops. And, I can see inside and decide if I want to grind a bit more or not.

Additionally, I like the fact that the grinder is quite compact and can sit on my counter without taking up too much room, plus is stainless steel, so looks good.

The best part is that the price is quite reasonable for a lightly used small appliance.

I would recommend this grinder to anyone who likes to grind their own beans for use in a French Press occasionally, but it may not be heavy duty enough for daily use (I'll tell you in another 3 years, because that would be the equivalent, I suppose!)time of deliveryIt's a great quality coffee grinder. I was penny pinching with the cheap Chinese grinders and those things are crazy. Some of them you have to turn the coffee beans upside down to grind them and crazy stuff like that. This is a great unitI really like this coffee grinder and have had this exact same model for years. Recently I noticed that I had a crack in the piece of plastic that holds the blade in the grinder so I bought this replacement for it when it finally dies since the product is discontinued and they want more for just the chopping bowl than the entire grinder itself.

For those that complain that it is messy etc, they are simply lazy.

Grind the coffee until you no longer hear grinding, and then shake the top before you remove it from over top the grinder.I love this coffee maker. I bought one years ago but this one was a gift for a relative. Mine has lasted for almost ten years. Theirs not so long. I recommend not washing the base -- just use a slightly damp paper towel to clean it. I do the same with the other parts.
reviews_1s:
I bought it just about a year and a half ago and first, the plastic parts of the grinder cup broke within a few months. I filled a claim with the manufacturer on the warranty and they sent me a new grinder cup after almost a month. Then in July, 2024 it just stopped working altogether. It had a 3 year warranty, so I filed a claim for the whole thing. Their customer Service would send me one email a week asking for one thing to provide information about it. Proof of purchase or model number, date of purchase, what exactly was I doing when it stopped working. Week after week after week! Then they wanted a picture of it with the cord cut off to be emailed to a totally different email address, and then in 10 working days I will get another one. I waited 15 working days to check in and they replied after 3 days to say they didn't get the pictures! So I sent the pictures again and told them I will check every day. I finally got the replacement coffee grinder THIS WEEK! SEPTEMBER 22ND! Cuisinart used to have good products, what happened?
NOT AMAZON'S FAULT!!! absolutely Cuisinart/Conair!!Delivered on October 7 2024. Quit working on November 20 2024. Grinder pulse button would stick on and you couldn’t stop it or pull the lid off. Probably used it three times a week, nothing extreme.
The only way to get it to stop grinding is to unplug it. By the time you pull the plug out of the wall the beans are ground to an extremely fine dust, wasted!
Phoned Amazon to get a refund or exchange and they told me to phone Cuisinart.
I’m without a coffee grinder…. Never has Amazon not helped!!!! Rated a one because it wouldn’t let me leave zero starsThis is the worst coffee grinder I have ever used. It takes forever to grind. It never comes close to grinding it. I bought it to long ago to return it.this is the second one of these units that has broken after about 16 months of use. the spinner/grinder breaks off from the connection to the motor.......this is the second and LAST one i will buy - avoid this product.....the brand name no longer represents qualityHave purchased Cuisinart Grind Central Coffee Grinder for years from Bed Bath & Beyond and had no problems with this coffee grinder. Since BBB closed, have purchased two of the same Grinder through Amazon and these most recent purchases didn't last - first one 5 months, second one only a month and now too late to return. Contacted Customer Service and I am suppose to receive a new grinder after I cut off the cord (strange) take a picture of cord & grinder & email it to Cuisinart but that doesn't help since there is obviously a problem with product development. Customer Service Rep not at all helpful and difficult to understand.

Would not buy this model again.I really liked this coffee grinder until it broke this week. I bought it less than six months ago and from the photo that I submitted, you can see that the blade broke. I had an old style Cuisinart coffee grinder that I had for many years and I had to throw it away because the cord frayed at the area it was attached to the grinder. I loved that one and thought that this one was great too until the blade broke. I am going to see if they stand by their products and if I can get another one but I doubt it!Three broken grinders, the last one only made it 4 months. No more purchases by Cuisinart.
And I really needed a cup of coffee.The grinder worked okay for the first few weeks, except for coffee grinds leaking out of the bottom under the cup. However, after about 8 weeks of minimal use, the cup stuck to the grinder and won’t come off at all. (Unfortunately the return date cut-off was in June, so am stuck with a useless machine).
I would not recommend it.While it looks nice and grinds coffee well, this product is a major safety hazard. After just a couple of uses the mechanism that engages the motor to grind seems to have broken. I tried to blow it out and make sure nothing was clogging it. This meant that upon plugging it in the grinder would turn on, even if the lid was off. My mom later said she had the same one a while ago and had the same issue. This is a major flaw. Additionally, it was not as quiet as I had hoped and the grounds spilled out of the cup a little bit while it was grinding - both would have been totally tolerable if the on off function worked. Will have to return it.This is garbage. Cuisinart used to be a reputable brand but their quality has gone down hill. I had one of these for years and it worked beautifully until it just didn't. It worked so well I replaced it with the same one. It broke in just a few months. I called in for warranty and they said they would replace it. I purchased another because I needed to have one in the mean time. The second one broke in exactly the same way as the first replacement only in less than a month. I called again. I was assured I would be sent two replacements and I would need to send my damaged items in. It has been 9 months! Now they want proof of purchase on one saying it is outside of warranty. It wasn't outside of warranty when I called 9 months ago! I will not purchase this brand again. Do yourself a favor and look elsewhere.
